rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Motion To Compel # 1) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Theodora Oringer Pc To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1 And Demands For Production, Set 1; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Theodora Oringher Pc And Its Counsel, In The Sum Of $2250.00 And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 1, 2012, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT MOHAMMAD CHAHINE (Motion To Compel # 1) Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Theodora Oringer Pc To Provide Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 1 And Demands For Production, Set 1; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Req. For Monetary Sanctions Against Theodora Oringher Pc And Its Counsel, In The Sum Of $2250.00 Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The court believes, based on the parties' submittals, that counsel have not fully executed their obligation to meet and confer about the disputed discovery items. The court requests that counsel for the parties meet and confer in person about the items in dispute prior to the hearing on Monday with a view to simplifying and minimizing the matters to be resolved by the court. The court will reserve its ruling on the motions until they are called on the calendar. The court will request that the parties advise the court, at that time, what progress they have made in resolving the discovery matters at issue.

rulings.Case Title: RAYNETTA RAYZETTA VS. RED AND WHITE FLEET et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Fisherman'S Wharf Bay Cruise Corporation Dba Red & White Fleet'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 1, 2012, Line 15, Defendant Fisherman's Wharf Bay Cruise Corporation dba Red & White Fleet's Motion For Summary Judgment. Hearing required due to plaintiff's contest of the tentative ruling. The tentative ruling is: Granted. Defendant satisfied its initial burden to show that plaintiff does not meet the test for seaman status and that any negligence by defendant was not in its vessel capacity and plaintiff has not presented any admissible evidence creating a triable issue on either of these points. Defendant's evidence objections are sustained. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ENRIQUE PEDROZA et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP, A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defts Subpoenas And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, October 1, 2012, Line 2, PLAINTIFF ENRIQUE PEDROZA, LAILA PORTUGAL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Defts Subpoenas And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied without prejudice for failure to deliver the required courtesy copies of the filed papers to the court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 1, 2012, Line 11, DEFENDANT JUDGE RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Continued on the court's own motion to October 16, 2012. Judge Kahn has recused himself from hearing any matters in this case. =(302/HEK)
